title: Fibonacci Reply Paper — Platform Cross-Validation Results
framework: Phase-Gated AR(2) for Colonic Crypt Renewal

amendments.original_claim: BMAL1-KO removes phase modulation but preserves |r|
amendments.amended_to: BMAL1-KO collapses the clock-target eigenvalue hierarchy, redistributing persistence between regulatory layers
amendments.evidence: GSE157357: gap shifts from +0.392 (WT) to -0.082 (BMAL1-KO); clock eigenvalue drops from 0.723 to ~0.45

amendments.original_claim: Tuft cells as sensitive readout of temporal coherence
amendments.amended_to: Tuft cells as delayed, accumulative readout of temporal coherence
amendments.evidence: DCLK1 |λ| = 0.9999 (near-critical); tuft lifespan ≥28 days; FOLFIRI overshoot is post-treatment not immediate

amendments.amended_to: Bidirectional tuft pattern: suppressed in CRC tumours, dramatically upregulated post-FOLFIRI
amendments.evidence: Nguyen 2025 Broad cohort + TCGA: tuft markers lower in tumours; Mzoughi 2025: most dramatic upregulation post-FOLFIRI

amendments.amended_to: φ-enrichment p = 0.154, not significant; Fibonacci connection is conceptual bridge, not statistical property
amendments.evidence: Genome-wide enrichment test on PAR(2) platform; individual genes Per2/Hes1/Wee1 near φ but not genome-wide significant

amendments.amended_to: Explicit terminological note distinguishing Boman's Mature dividing cells from microfold (M) cells
amendments.evidence: Nguyen 2025 defines M cells as PGLYRP1+/GP2+ microfold cells; Boman's model uses M for Mature dividing cells

amendments.original_claim: No citation of Nguyen, Lausten and Boman 2025
amendments.amended_to: Full integration of Nguyen 2025 review: NOTCH-tuft suppression, WNT gradient, DCS niche, M-cell terminology, bidirectional tuft CRC data
amendments.evidence: Cells 2025;14(18):1428, co-authored by Boman, published 6 weeks before original Reply submission

claim_verification.claim: AR(2) is minimum sufficient model for oscillatory gene expression
claim_verification.platform_evidence: AR(2) preferred by AIC in ~70-80% of genes across 8+ datasets and >10,000 genes

claim_verification.claim: Lgr5, Arntl, Per2, Axin2 show stable complex AR(2) roots with |r| < 1
claim_verification.platform_evidence: All four genes confirmed stable in GSE157357; eigenvalues vary by tissue context
claim_verification.caveat: Specific values are snapshot from single organoid condition; eigenvalues vary substantially by tissue

claim_verification.claim: BMAL1/CLOCK is the circadian phase source (Layer 3)
claim_verification.platform_evidence: 85 significant BMAL1 coupling events across 33 genes in 12 tissues; Wee1 coupled in 10/12 tissues; 180x enrichment over random controls; BMAL1-KO collapses hierarchy

claim_verification.claim: NOTCH acts as the fast memory kernel α₁
claim_verification.platform_evidence: Hes1 |λ| = 0.619 (Hypothalamus, GSE54650); NOTCH pathway genes tracked in annotations; biology confirms fast binary switch
claim_verification.missing: No NOTCH-KO time-series dataset for direct AR(2) perturbation test

claim_verification.claim: WNT/AXIN2 acts as the slow/delayed kernel α₂
claim_verification.platform_evidence: APC-KO (constitutive WNT) inverts eigenvalue hierarchy: gap from +0.392 to -0.122; target persistence exceeds clock persistence when WNT permanently on
BMAL1-KO substantially changes eigenvalues: clock drops from 0.723 to ~0.45, targets increase from 0.331 to ~0.53; hierarchy collapses, not just phase removed
claim_verification.amended_prediction: BMAL1-KO collapses the clock-target hierarchy; phase source required for maintaining differential persistence

claim_verification.platform_evidence: No AXIN2-KO dataset available; closest proxy is APC-KO (opposite direction)

claim_verification.claim: Tuft cells as sensitive readout of temporal coherence
claim_verification.platform_evidence: DCLK1 |λ| = 0.9999 (near-critical, highest of all cell type markers); consistent with 28+ day lifespan; but near-critical eigenvalue implies slow dynamics, not rapid sensitivity
claim_verification.amended_interpretation: Tuft fraction is a delayed, accumulative readout — perturbations take many cycles to manifest as measurable tuft changes

claim_verification.claim: Chemotherapy → transient eigenvalue shift → delayed tuft overshoot
claim_verification.platform_evidence: Nguyen 2025 reports FOLFIRI causes most dramatic upregulation of tuft cells post-treatment (Broad cohort, TCGA, Mzoughi 2025 Figures 3d, 5a-b). Not directly tested with AR(2) on chemotherapy time-series
claim_verification.bidirectional_pattern: Tuft markers LOWER in primary CRC tumours, HIGHER post-FOLFIRI

claim_verification.claim: DCS cells implement PAR(2) memory kernels
claim_verification.platform_evidence: REG4+ DCS cells confirmed as niche-supporting at crypt base (Nguyen 2025); convergence map links to kernel components; no DCS-specific time-series eigenvalues

22 datasets, 5 species, 21,000+ genes genome-wide; hierarchy preserved in 12/12 mouse tissues, 3/3 human conditions, 3/3 Arabidopsis replicates, 4/8 baboon tissues; bias-audited with 3 automated tests
Genome-wide enrichment test p = 0.154; individual genes Per2 (0.619), Hes1 (0.619), Wee1 (0.615) remarkably close to 1/φ = 0.618, but genome-wide not significant

ode_model_zoo_validation.description: AR(2) correctly captures eigenvalue structure of 5 canonical biological oscillator ODE models

non_circadian_generalization.key_finding: Regulator > effector hierarchy preserved: fast responders (Il1b |λ|=0.55, Junb |λ|=0.43) < sustained effectors (Stat1 |λ|=0.99, Ifit1 |λ|=0.80)
non_circadian_generalization.relevance: Eigenvalue hierarchy is not circadian-specific; generalises to any system with regulatory layers

nguyen_2025_integration.citation: Nguyen AL, Lausten MA, Boman BM. Cells 2025;14(18):1428
nguyen_2025_integration.gap_identified: Original Reply did not cite this review despite Boman co-authorship and direct relevance
findings_integrated: NOTCH promotes absorptive fate, inhibits secretory (including tuft) via HES1→ATOH1 — supports fast kernel α₁
WNT maintains stemness via mesenchymal spatial gradient, highest at crypt base — supports slow kernel α₂
REG4+ DCS/Paneth-like cells provide niche factors at crypt base — supports DCS as kernel components
Tuft cells <0.5% of epithelium, DCLK1+/POU2AF2+ — supports rarity/sensitivity argument
Tuft cells LOWER in CRC tumours (Broad cohort, TCGA) — new finding, not in original Reply
Tuft cells dramatically UPREGULATED post-FOLFIRI (most dramatic of all cell types) — validates Prediction 3
EECs also upregulated post-FOLFIRI — tuft signal is not isolated, multiple secretory lineages shift
M cells = microfold cells (PGLYRP1+/GP2+), distinct from Boman's Mature dividing cells — terminology flagged
Cellular plasticity (LGR5+/LGR5- interconversion, fetal program reactivation) — challenge for stable-identity models
